claim: The TIGER2020 TABBLOCK20 directory at https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/TIGER2020/TABBLOCK20/ exists and is publicly accessible on the Census FTP mirror.
quote: Index of /geo/tiger/TIGER2020/TABBLOCK20 - Census.gov

claim: Per-state TABBLOCK20 download files follow the naming pattern tl_2020_<2-digit-FIPS>_tabblock20.zip, e.g. tl_2020_25_tabblock20.zip for Massachusetts (FIPS 25).
quote: Census — https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/TIGER2020/TABBLOCK20/tl_2020_25_tabblock20.zip

claim: The TABBLOCK20 DBF record layout contains the field GEOID20, defined as the Census block identifier — a concatenation of the 2020 Census state FIPS code, county FIPS code, census tract code, and census block number.
quote: GEOID20 (15 characters, String) - Census block identifier; a concatenation of 2020 Census state FIPS code, 2020 Census county FIPS code, 2020 Census tract code, and 2020 Census block number

claim: The TABBLOCK20 DBF record layout contains the field POP20, representing the 2020 Census population count for the census block.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: The TABBLOCK20 DBF record layout contains the field HOUSING20, representing the 2020 Census housing unit count for the census block.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: The TABBLOCK20 DBF record layout contains the field ALAND20, representing the 2020 Census land area (in square meters) of the census block.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: The TABBLOCK20 DBF record layout contains the field AWATER20, representing the 2020 Census water area (in square meters) of the census block.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: The TABBLOCK20 DBF record layout contains the field INTPTLAT20, storing the latitude of the internal point of the census block as a signed decimal-degree string.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: The TABBLOCK20 DBF record layout contains the field INTPTLON20, storing the longitude of the internal point of the census block as a signed decimal-degree string.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: The 2020 cartographic state boundary file (1:500,000 scale) is located at https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/GENZ2020/shp/cb_2020_us_state_500k.zip.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: INTPTLAT20 and INTPTLON20 store the internal point coordinates as signed decimal-degree character strings (not numeric), with a leading sign character for direction.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: The U.S. House of Representatives has a fixed total of 435 seats apportioned among the 50 states following the 2020 Census.
quote: Apportionment is the process of dividing the 435 memberships, or seats, in the U.S. House of Representatives among the 50 states.

claim: Colorado gained one seat in the 2020 Census apportionment, bringing its total to 8 House seats effective for the 2022–2030 elections.
quote: Texas will gain two seats in the House of Representatives, five states will gain one seat each (Colorado, Florida, Montana, North Carolina, and Oregon)

claim: New York lost one seat in the 2020 Census apportionment, bringing its total to 26 House seats effective for the 2022–2030 elections.
quote: seven states will lose one seat each (California, Illinois, Michigan, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, and West Virginia)

claim: Montana gained one seat in the 2020 Census apportionment, regaining its second House seat and bringing its total to 2, effective for the 2022–2030 elections.
quote: five states will gain one seat each (Colorado, Florida, Montana, North Carolina, and Oregon)

claim: Exactly six states have a single at-large House district following 2020 apportionment: Alaska, Delaware, North Dakota, South Dakota, Vermont, and Wyoming.
value: AK, DE, ND, SD, VT, WY
quote: Each of the 50 states is entitled to a minimum of one seat in the U.S. House of Representatives.

claim: The complete 2020 Census apportionment allocates 435 House seats across all 50 states as shown in the compact JSON value.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: Colorado's 2020 Census resident population (PL 94-171 block-sum count, 50 states + DC + PR table) is 5,773,714.
quote: The Centennial State's population was 5,773,714 in 2020, making it one of 13 states and the District of Columbia that grew by 10.0% or more from 2010 to 2020.

claim: Colorado's 2020 Census apportionment population (resident population plus overseas federal military/civilian personnel allocable to Colorado) is 5,782,171.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: Colorado's state FIPS code is 08.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: Colorado's total land area is approximately 103,642 square miles per the 2020 Census.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: New York's 2020 Census resident population (PL 94-171 block-sum count) is 20,201,249.
quote: NO QUOTE FOUND

claim: The 2020 Census apportionment population equals the resident population plus overseas U.S. military and federal civilian employees (and their dependents) allocable to a home state; this overseas add-on is never used for census-block sum checks.
value: Apportionment Population = Resident Population + Overseas Population
quote: The population counts used for apportionment include the total resident population for each of the 50 states, plus a count of U.S. military and federal civilian employees living overseas (and their dependents living with them) who could be allocated to a home state.

claim: The npm package 'shapefile' (mbostock/mapbox) exposes openDbf() for reading .dbf attribute tables standalone, separate from the shapefile, and accepts a file path string, ArrayBuffer/Uint8Array buffer, or a Node/WhatWG readable stream.
quote: shapefile.openDbf(dbf[, options]) Returns a promise that yields a GeoJSON properties object source. Unlike shapefile.open, this only reads the dBASE file, and never the associated shapefile.

claim: shapefile.openDbf() accepts a file path string as its first argument to open a .dbf file from disk.
quote: If typeof dbf is "string", opens the dBASE at the specified dbf path.

claim: shapefile.openDbf() accepts an ArrayBuffer or Uint8Array as its first argument to read an in-memory .dbf.
quote: If dbf instanceof ArrayBuffer or dbf instanceof Uint8Array, reads the specified in-memory shapefile.

claim: shapefile.openDbf() accepts a Node readable stream or a WhatWG standard readable stream as its first argument.
value: Node readable stream / WhatWG readable stream
quote: Otherwise, dbf must be a Node readable stream in Node or a WhatWG standard readable stream in browsers.

claim: The npm package 'proj4' does NOT ship EPSG:5070 (NAD83/Conus Albers) as a built-in named definition; only a limited set of EPSG codes are included by default.
quote: EPSG:4326, which has the following alias 'WGS84' ... EPSG:4269 ... EPSG:3857 ... EPSG:32601 to EPSG:32660 ... EPSG:32701 to EPSG:32760 ... EPSG:5041 ... EPSG:5042

claim: The npm package 'proj4' does NOT ship EPSG:3338 (NAD83/Alaska Albers) as a built-in named definition.
quote: EPSG:4326, which has the following alias 'WGS84' ... EPSG:4269 ... EPSG:3857 ... EPSG:32601 to EPSG:32660 ... EPSG:32701 to EPSG:32760 ... EPSG:5041 ... EPSG:5042

claim: Custom projections not built into proj4 must be registered at runtime via proj4.defs(), which accepts a name and a projection string.
quote: proj4.defs method which can be called 2 ways, with a name and projection

claim: The authoritative PROJ.4 string for EPSG:5070 (NAD83/Conus Albers) from epsg.io is '+proj=aea +lat_0=23 +lon_0=-96 +lat_1=29.5 +lat_2=45.5 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=GRS80 +towgs84=0,0,0,0,0,0,0 +units=m +no_defs +type=crs'; the claimed string '+datum=NAD83' differs from the canonical epsg.io form which uses '+ellps=GRS80 +towgs84=0,0,0,0,0,0,0'.
quote: +proj=aea +lat_0=23 +lon_0=-96 +lat_1=29.5 +lat_2=45.5 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +ellps=GRS80 +towgs84=0,0,0,0,0,0,0 +units=m +no_defs +type=crs

claim: The npm package 'adm-zip' is a pure JavaScript implementation for zip data compression for Node.js with no native dependencies.
quote: ADM-ZIP is a pure JavaScript implementation for zip data compression for NodeJS

claim: adm-zip has no other Node.js library dependencies.
quote: There are no other nodeJS libraries that ADM-ZIP is dependent of

claim: adm-zip can extract an individual named entry from a zip archive to a target path on disk via zip.extractEntryTo().
quote: zip.extractEntryTo(/*entry name*/ "some_folder/my_file.txt", /*target path*/ "/home/me/tempfolder", /*maintainEntryPath*/ false, /*overwrite*/ true)

claim: The package '@turf/boolean-point-in-polygon' is installable as a standalone npm package independent of the full @turf/turf bundle.
quote: Install this single module individually: $ npm install @turf/boolean-point-in-polygon

claim: @turf/boolean-point-in-polygon tests a point against both Polygon and MultiPolygon GeoJSON geometry types.
quote: Takes a Point and a Polygon or MultiPolygon and determines if the point resides inside

claim: @turf/boolean-point-in-polygon works for both convex and concave polygons.
quote: determines if the point resides inside the polygon. The polygon can be convex or concave.
